Title: Effect of triple-frequency sono-germination and soaking treatments on techno-functional characteristics of barley

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GABA-T activity increased after TFUT, GE, TFUT+GE, and TS; it is implicated in GABA catabolism and overall GABA metabolism during treatment-induced accumulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enzyme activity increased in treated seeds, highest under TFUT+GE
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GABA-T activity increased significantly after all treatments, especially TFUT+GE. 2. The enzyme functions in GABA degradation after GABA synthesis. 3. GABA accumulation in treated barley depends on the balance between GAD and GABA-T activities.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although increased, GABA-T is catabolic rather than biosynthetic; included because it was a direct experimental focus affecting GABA metabolism.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GABA-T activity assay; HPLC quantification of GABA; treatment comparison
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for tuning GABA turnover in germinated barley processing
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May reduce net GABA if catabolic flux exceeds biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Polyphenolic compounds; total phenolics; total flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to form trans-cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for phenolic acids and flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation under GE, TFUT+GE, and TS is associated with enhanced accumulation of polyphenolic compounds in barley seeds; also upregulated by TFUT in both varieties
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point; entry enzyme of pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Measured in barley seeds after treatments; increased under GE, TFUT+GE, and TS in both varieties; increased under TFUT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seeds under TFUT, GE, TFUT+GE, TS relative to UB
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Triple-frequency ultrasound treatment; germination; sono-germination; traditional soaking
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL was one of the phenylpropanoid-pathway genes quantified in treated barley seeds. 2. PAL expression increased under GE, TFUT+GE, and TS in both ZQ2000 and XMLY22, and also increased under TFUT. 3. Its upregulation was linked to enhanced accumulation of polyphenolic compounds after treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper measured transcript changes and metabolite accumulation correlations; no direct transgenic manipulation of PAL was performed.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling of phenolic acids/flavonoids; treatment comparison
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker/target for boosting barley phenolics during germination-based processing or metabolic engineering of phenylpropanoid accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trans-cinnamic acid to produce p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early precursor for phenolic acid and flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression under treatments, especially GE, TFUT+GE, and TS, is associated with increased polyphenolic accumulation; upregulated by TFUT in both varieties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In seeds, increased under GE, TFUT+GE, TS in both varieties; also upregulated by TFUT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seeds after treatment relative to untreated barley
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Triple-frequency ultrasound treatment; germination; sono-germination; soaking
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H expression was monitored as part of treatment-responsive phenylpropanoid metabolism. 2. C4H was upregulated in both varieties under GE, TFUT+GE, and TS, and also under TFUT. 3. Increased C4H expression correlated with accumulation of identified phenolic acids and flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Association based on expression-metabolite response across treatments.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for increasing flux into polyphenol biosynthesis in barley grains

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Activation of hydroxycinnamates to CoA thioesters
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids to CoA esters, supplying intermediates for downstream phenylpropanoid products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursors for flavonoid and related phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased expression under GE, TFUT+GE, and TS, and also upregulated by TFUT, is associated with higher accumulation of polyphenolic compounds in treated barley
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in phenylpropanoid flux
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Seed expression increased under GE, TFUT+GE, TS in both varieties; upregulated by TFUT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seeds after treatment relative to UB
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL was treatment-responsive in both barley varieties. 2. GE, TFUT+GE, and TS increased 4CL expression, and TFUT also upregulated it. 3. Its induction coincided with enhanced phenolic acid/flavonoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential intervention point to increase grain phenylpropanoid content

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase, chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses 4-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone, the entry compound for flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursor for flavonoid products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CHS expression after treatments is associated with increased flavonoid and overall polyphenolic accumulation in barley seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in both varieties under GE, TFUT+GE, and TS; also upregulated by TFUT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seeds after treatment relative to UB
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS expression increased under all productive treatments, including TFUT, GE, TFUT+GE, and TS. 2. This increase paralleled elevated total flavonoid content and identified flavonoid compounds. 3. CHS is part of the treatment-responsive pathway underlying enhanced barley bioactive composition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study inferred pathway activation from transcript accumulation and flavonoid profiling, not from enzyme assays.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; TFC measurement; HPLC phenolic/flavonoid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for enhancing barley flavonoid content in food processing and breeding

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones to form dihydroflavonols for downstream flavonol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In ZQ2000, TFUT upregulated F3H; in XMLY22, TFUT downregulated F3H; GE, TFUT+GE, and TS increased F3H in both varieties, associating with higher flavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Treatment-responsive in seeds; cultivar-dependent response under TFUT, but increased under GE, TFUT+GE, and TS in both varieties
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F3H showed genotype-dependent response to TFUT: induced in ZQ2000 but repressed in XMLY22. 2. GE, TFUT+GE, and TS increased F3H expression in both varieties. 3. These expression patterns were associated with enhanced flavonoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the few genes showing opposite TFUT response between the two varieties.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for flavonoid enhancement, but genotype-specific treatment responses should be considered

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces 3-hydroxyl group into hydroxycinnamate intermediates to form caffeoyl-type compounds
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms intermediates for caffeic, chlorogenic, and ferulic acid-related phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TFUT downregulated C3H in both varieties, while GE, TFUT+GE, and TS increased expression, correlating with enhanced polyphenolic accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Decreased under TFUT in ZQ2000 and XMLY22; increased under GE, TFUT+GE, and TS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C3H was suppressed by TFUT in both barley varieties. 2. GE, TFUT+GE, and TS reversed this pattern and increased C3H expression. 3. Induction of C3H under productive treatments correlated with increased phenolic compound accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper labels C3H in the measured gene list but does not provide accession or exact enzyme standardization.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for increasing caffeic/chlorogenic/ferulic-related metabolites in barley grain

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups from CoA esters to shikimate or quinate acceptors in phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies intermediates for downstream caffeoyl/chlorogenic and related phenylpropanoid compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HCT was downregulated by TFUT in both varieties but upregulated by GE, TFUT+GE, and TS, consistent with enhanced phenolic accumulation under the latter treatments
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Decreased under TFUT in ZQ2000 and XMLY22; increased under GE, TFUT+GE, and TS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HCT transcript abundance decreased after TFUT alone in both varieties. 2. GE, TFUT+GE, and TS increased HCT expression. 3. HCT expression changes tracked treatment-dependent enhancement of polyphenolic compounds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional role inferred from pathway annotation and transcript-metabolite association.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for optimizing hydroxycinnamate-derived nutritional metabolites in barley processing

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms flavonol products contributing to total flavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FLS expression was increased by TFUT in both varieties and also increased under GE, TFUT+GE, and TS, corresponding to greater flavonoid/flavonol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated under TFUT, GE, TFUT+GE, and TS in both varieties
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLS was consistently induced by the treatments that enhanced grain flavonoids. 2. Both varieties showed FLS upregulation under TFUT and under GE, TFUT+GE, and TS. 3. This induction aligned with increased flavonoid/flavonol accumulation in barley seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study particularly links gene-expression changes with identified flavonoid compounds such as rutin and kaempferol.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for increasing flavonol-rich barley ingredients

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive metabolite linked to health-promoting/antidiabetic potential and stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates glutamate to produce gamma-aminobutyric acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Treatment-induced increase in GAD activity, especially under TFUT+GE, is associated with elevated GABA accumulation in barley seeds
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as rate-limiting enzyme in the GABA shunt by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Enzyme activity increased after TFUT, GE, TFUT+GE, and TS, with highest increase under TFUT+GE
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAD was identified as the rate-limiting enzyme of the GABA shunt. 2. GAD activity increased after TFUT, GE, TS, and most strongly after TFUT+GE in both varieties. 3. Increased GAD activity was associated with elevated GABA accumulation in treated barley seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study assayed enzyme activity rather than transcript abundance for GAD.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GAD activity assay; HPLC quantification of GABA; treatment comparison
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzymatic target for producing GABA-enriched germinated barley foods
